questions.text: I respond to new information by pivoting quickly rather than updating a formal plan.
My startle response is pronounced — unexpected sounds or events jolt me intensely.
I believe that my calm disposition is simply how my system is calibrated.
I find that directing my energy inward—toward my own improvement—is deeply satisfying.
I am usually focused on the substance of a discussion rather than its emotional texture.
I feel most at ease when I have minimal systems and maximum freedom.
I believe that remaining emotionally uninvolved during another's crisis enables a quality of judgment that emotional involvement would compromise.
I believe that my steady temperament reflects a genuine way of experiencing the world.
I am the person who helps refine and improve someone else's plan of action.
I feel that acquiring resources for my own future is prudent and right.
I hold the view that taking others' suffering seriously enough to act on it — not just to notice it — is what moral conviction actually means.
I notice the subtle ways people claim or yield conversational space.
I feel that collaborative decision-making leads to more durable outcomes.
I believe indecision is more dangerous than a wrong decision.
I find that analytical clarity during emotionally demanding situations has been the quality that most consistently enabled me to achieve outcomes I can be proud of.
I find that stepping back so others can step forward is one of the most meaningful things I do.
I find that I am genuinely puzzled when people tell me I missed an obvious social cue.
I create and follow budgets for my personal and professional life.
